Title: MVMM: Multiview Multimodal 3-D Object Detection for Autonomous Driving
Author: Li, Shangjie and Geng, Keke and Yin, Guodong and Wang, Ziwei and Qian, Min
Keywords: 3-D object detection, autonomous driving, multimodal fusion, multiview framework, point clouds coloring, Three-dimensional displays, Point cloud compression, Feature extraction, Object detection, Laser radar, Proposals, Image color analysis, Object Detection, Autonomous Vehicles, Feature Maps, Point Cloud, Semantic Information, 3D Space, Geometric Information, Bird's Eye, KITTI Dataset, Detection Head, Convolutional Layers, Data Augmentation, Bounding Box, RGB Images, Fusion Method, Color Information, Pedestrian Detection, Feature-level Fusion, Global Rotation, Fusion Strategy
Abstract: Object detection in 3-D space is a fundamental technology in the autonomous driving system. Among the published 3-D object detection methods, the single-modal methods based on point clouds have been widely studied. One problem exposed by these methods is that point clouds lack color and texture features. The limitation in conveying semantic information often leads to failures in detection. In contrast, the multimodal methods based on the image and point clouds fusion may solve this problem, but relevant research is not sufficient. In this work, a single-stage multiview multimodal 3-D object detector (MVMM) is proposed, which can naturally and efficiently extract semantic and geometric information from the image and point clouds. Specifically, the data-level fusion approach of point clouds coloring is used for combining information from the camera and LIDAR. Next, an encoder-decoder backbone is devised to extract features from colored points in the range view. Then, colored points are concatenated with the range view features, voxelized, and fed into the point view bridge for down-sampling. Finally, the down-sampled feature map is used by the bird's eye view backbone and the detection head for generating 3-D results based on predefined anchors. According to extensive experiments on the KITTI dataset, MVMM achieves competitive performance while runs at 27 FPS on the 1080 Ti GPU. Particularly, MVMM performs extremely well in difficult scenes (e.g., heavy occlusion and truncation) due to the understanding of fused information.
Field of Study: machine learning, deep learning, sensor fusion, and robotics/autonomous systems

Title: MS2CANet: Multiscale Spatial–Spectral Cross-Modal Attention Network for Hyperspectral Image and LiDAR Classification
Author: Xianghai Wang; Junheng Zhu; Yining Feng; Lu Wang
Publication Title: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
Keywords: hyperspectral image classification, LiDAR, multiscale feature extraction, cross-modal attention, deep learning, remote sensing
Abstract: The acquisition of multisource remote-sensing (RS) data has become more convenient due to the boom and innovation of RS imaging technology. The fusion and classification of hyperspectral images (HSIs) and LiDAR data has become a research hotspot because of their excellent complementarity and the vigorous development of deep learning provides effective methods. Most existing CNN-based methods have fixed convolution kernels, making it difficult to extract multiscale detailed features. This paper proposes a multiscale pyramid fusion framework based on spatial–spectral cross-modal attention (S2CA) for HSIs and LiDAR classification, which has strong multiscale information learning ability, especially in areas with complex information changes. Multiscale pyramid convolution is used to extract multiscale features, and an effective feature recalibration (EFR) module is used to enhance features and suppress useless information at each scale. An S2CA module uses features of different modes to enhance each other. Three real public datasets are used for experiments, and the proposed method achieves the best results compared to existing advanced methods.
Field of Study: Remote Sensing, Computer Vision, Deep Learning
DataFusionClassificationReason: Fuses two distinct remote sensing modalities — hyperspectral imagery (HSI) and LiDAR — using a cross-modal attention mechanism to improve land cover classification accuracy.

Title: Integrating Multi-Omics Data With EHR for Precision Medicine Using Advanced Artificial Intelligence
Author: Li Tong; Wenqi Shi; Monica Isgut; Yishan Zhong; Peter Lais; Logan Gloster; Jimin Sun; Aniketh Swain; Felipe Giuste; May D. Wang
Keywords: Multi-omics; electronic health records; data integration; artificial intelligence; precision medicine; radiogenomics; EHR; deep learning; biobank; federated learning
Abstract: With the recent advancement of novel biomedical technologies such as high-throughput sequencing and wearable devices, multi-modal biomedical data ranging from multi-omics molecular data to real-time continuous bio-signals are generated at an unprecedented speed. In this review, the authors summarize state-of-the-art AI models for integrating multi-omics data and electronic health records (EHRs) for precision medicine, covering modality-specific learning for multi-omics and EHR data, cross-modality integration (radiogenomics and EHR-omics), and challenges including data harmonization, population heterogeneity, data quality, and high dimensionality. Future directions include federated learning, transfer learning, fairness-aware ML, and explainable AI.
Field of Study: Biomedical Engineering; Bioinformatics; Precision Medicine; Machine Learning
DataFusionClassificationReason: Reviews AI-based methods for integrating multiple biomedical data modalities (multi-omics + EHR) for precision medicine; covers early, mixed, intermediate, hierarchical fusion frameworks, radiogenomics pipelines, and phenotype/genotype-first EHR-omics integration.

Title: Improving RGB-infrared object detection with cascade alignment-guided transformer
Author: Maoxun Yuan, Xiaorong Shi, Nan Wang, Yinyan Wang, Xingxing Wei
Keywords: RGB-IR object detection; multispectral fusion; image misalignment; feature fusion; aerial object detection; transformer; region of interest
Abstract: The paper proposes a Cascade Alignment-Guided Transformer (CAGT) detection head to address weak misalignment and fusion imprecision in RGB-infrared object detection. It introduces a Translation-Scale-Rotation Alignment (TSRA) module to calibrate cross-modal RoI features and a Complementary Fusion Transformer (CFT) module to capture complementary cross-modal features. These are evaluated on the DroneVehicle and KAIST datasets.
Field of Study: Computer Vision, Object Detection, Multispectral Imaging
DataFusionClassificationReason: Fuses RGB (visible) and infrared image modalities using a dedicated RoI detection head; evaluated on two separate datasets (DroneVehicle and KAIST).

Title: Efficient multi-branch dynamic fusion network for super-resolution of industrial component image
Author: Guanqiang Wang, Mingsong Chen, Y.C. Lin, Xianhua Tan, Chizhou Zhang, Wenxin Yao, Baihui Gao, Kai Li, Zehao Li, Weidong Zeng
Keywords: super-resolution, CNN, Transformer, parallel fusion, dynamic edge attention, industrial image, welding, lightweight network, WCI110
Abstract: This work aims to promote the application of a high-performance super-resolution (SR) method in industry. Considering the lack of industrial datasets to evaluate performance, an industrial image SR dataset called WCI110 is first established, comprising 110 typical welding component images with 2040 × 1524 pixels. Subsequently, a parallel fusion structure of CNN and Transformer (FPFCT) is designed to achieve a high-quality reconstruction of component images. This model mainly contains the flexible parallel fusion (FPF) block and dynamic edge attention (DEA) network. The irreparable information loss caused by the inheritable sampling imperfection of CNN and Transformer can be effectively avoided by the parallel fusion structure in FPF block, while the contour features of multi-scale targets can be dynamically enhanced by DEA modules. FPFCT achieves more than 37.5% reduction in model parameters compared to SwinIR, and more than 48.9% reduction in single-image delay time.
Field of Study: Computer Vision, Industrial Inspection, Image Processing
DataFusionClassificationReason: The paper performs feature-level fusion by combining CNN-extracted local spatial features and Transformer-extracted global features in a parallel architecture (FPFCT). The FPF block explicitly fuses two independent feature extraction streams, and the DEA module further integrates multi-scale edge features — constituting a multi-source information fusion approach for image super-resolution.
